name: UTM Link Creator & QR Code Generator with Scheduled Google Analytics Reports
description: Automated workflow: UTM Link Creator & QR Code Generator with Scheduled Google Analytics Reports. This workflow integrates 13 different services: stickyNote, googleAnalyticsTool, httpRequest, airtable, code. It contains 18 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Google Analytics Data Analysis Agent

options.systemMessage:
"You are an advanced data analytics AI specializing in executive reporting. Your task is to analyze the provided dataset and generate a structured executive summary that highlights key insights, trends, and actionable takeaways. Structure your summary in the following format:

Overview – Briefly describe the dataset and its significance.
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) – Highlight the most important metrics and compare them to previous periods if applicable.
Trends & Insights – Identify patterns, growth areas, declines, and anomalies.
Opportunities & Recommendations – Provide strategic recommendations based on the insights.
Conclusion – Summarize the key takeaways concisely.
*Ensure the tone is professional, clear, and tailored for executives who require quick, data-driven insights without unnecessary details."

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
Create a marketing link with UTM parameters. Easily store in database and have QR code created and ready as well.

Type in requirements:
website URL
campaign id
campaign source
campaign medium
campaign name
campaign term

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
Code node creates the URL with UTM parameters. 

It then sends to your Airtable database to store for records. It also creates a QR code with the embedded link to be used for materials. 

Sample Airtable Setup:
-Website Link UTM column

parameters.content:

*Sample Google Report Metrics: Sessions. Update metrics as needed.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Create UTM Link & Send To Database".
Open the scheduler node "Schedule Google Analytics Report To Marketing Manager".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Analytics" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleAnalyticsTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Submit UTM Link To Database" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create QR Code With Submitted QR Link" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Analytics Data Analysis Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Summary Report To Marketing Manager" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set UTM Parameters For Link".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
